content:
There is nothing in Thunderbird that would selectively block messages like that. You can intentionally create filters that can delete messages according to criteria that you specify. This seems unlikely because you would know if you had created such filters, but have you checked the Deleted folder?

Otherwise, the first sanity check would be to watch your Comcast web mail page. If the missing messages are visible, then we'd have to figure out if/why they are not being downloaded by Thunderbird. If they never show up there, then Thunderbird would never know about them.

You don't say how long this has been going on, but I have seen it happen where some sender's mail has gotten stuck in their outgoing queue for whatever technical reason, but they eventually get released and arrive at their destination. Similarly, Comcast could be having a technical issue and some emails may be stuck in their system for a while before being released.

If a sender's email can't be delivered after a period of time (could be hours or days,) they usually get a server-generated non-delivery message. Have your senders received anything like that?

I think it's a Comcast issue.
creator: rednoise

content:
Steve Pecoraro said
I have followed all instructions I can find through many searches online but cannot get my outlook email to configure in Thunderbird. First, I have no option to chose OAuth2 even though I have deleted any outlook passwords, email accounts, and Thunderbird is up to date. I have received so many error messages I cant keep track. Bottom line, all my other email accounts configure find except for outlook. Any help would be appreciated Steven Pecoraro

Lets look at this seriously. Thunderbird knows the correct settings for outlook.com and office365, including the requirement for the use of oAuth2.0 so I can only guess there is more to this story than you have shared. I have seen reports that Microsoft are having issues with exchange authentication on their end over the past few weeks. as email is an exchange hosted product issue with authentication may be relevant

What version of Thunderbird? Versions before 140 struggle with the ever changing Microsoft requirements and really need to be updated to at least the ESR version. What domain is the address on? ie Outlook.com, hotmail.com etc. Do you have an antivirus other than Windows defender. Everything else has been known to prevent the successful completion of the new account wizard. Do you have a VPN? Do you still have issues without it? Now the most important question of all. What error do you receive that lead you to think the setup is not successful?

content:
Sounds like something is interfering and the most common culprit is an Anti-Virus program.

It may be scanning incoming mail before it allow Thunderbird access and/or scanning any file that gets opened in the profile before anything can get written to a file etc. It can cause all sorts of performance issues.

Compacting means Thunderbird creates a new mbox file eg: Inbox- it then opens the current Inbox mbox file and reads it - anything that does not have a 'marked as deleted' flag meaning anything previous either deleted or moved - gets copied to the new file. Then when completed, the original is deleted and the new Inbox replaces it. This is repeated for all your mbox files = folders as seen in Thunderbird.

When Thunderbird wants to compact all folders - it is advisable to do the following. Put Thunderbird in Offline mode, thus ensuring nothing is incoming, do not select any folder or email or create any emails - just wait and under no circumstance exit/close Thunderbird. The whole process should not take more than a few moments. If you are just doing a manual compact on one folder eg: Inbox, then it's likely to be a couple of seconds. So going offline should not be necessary.

What Anti-Virus are you using ? Advise is to access Anti-Virus and set up an exception to not scan your Thunderbird profile folder or anything it contains.

If you downloaded Thunderbird from the official website: https://www.thunderbird.net/en-US/thunderbird/all/

then the 'Thunderbird' profile folder to exclude from scans is here; C://Users/username/Appdata/Roaming/Thunderbird
creator: Toad-Hall
